title: carex capillaris subsp. krausei
intro: Carex capillaris subsp. krausei, also known as Carex krausei, is a subspecies of sedge, a type of flowering plant that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. It is native to various regions, including Asia and Europe, and has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. Carex capillaris subsp. krausei can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions, which may be used to promote respiratory health and relieve congestion.
